commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the oc-path plugin to normalize slash-deep JSON/JSONC paths, add `--value-json` leaf replacement, reject non-finite parsed JSON values, and document/test the new behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and v2026.5.22 source reject the linked slash-deep `oc://openclaw.json/...` path with `OC_PATH_TOO_DEEP`; I did not execute the CLI because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: No narrow automated code repair is indicated; the remaining blockers are maintainer approval of the public oc-path grammar/flag change and contributor real-behavior proof.

target.title: Fix oc-path deep config edits
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85929.

This makes  usable for the nested  edits Omar hit during config repair:

- JSON/JSONC file slots can now use slash-deep paths like ; the parser normalizes those extra slash segments into the existing dotted-subsegment JSON resolver model.
- Non-JSON file kinds keep the existing four structural slots, so markdown/YAML semantics do not change.
-  now supports  for explicit shape-changing JSON leaf replacements, such as replacing a string SecretRef shorthand with a structured SecretRef object.
- Parsed JSON values reject non-finite numbers before they become JSONC/JSONL AST values.
-  documents both deep JSON paths and .

## Verification

- [ERR_PNPM_ABORTED_REMOVE_MODULES_DIR_NO_TTY] Aborted removal of modules directory due to no TTY

If you are running pnpm in CI, set the CI environment variable to "true", or set "confirmModulesPurge" to "false".

If you are running pnpm in CI, set the CI environment variable to "true", or set "confirmModulesPurge" to "false".

Known gap: error TS5058: The specified path does not exist: '/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/tsconfig.core.json'. currently fails on  /  before this oc-path change ( exported inferred type issue). I did not change those files.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:  no longer rejects slash-deep  paths with , and it can explicitly replace JSONC leaves with parsed JSON objects using .

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 OpenClaw worktree with local package install.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: 

Evidence after fix: focused tests cover , CLI writes through a slash-deep config path,  string-to-object replacement for SecretRef-shaped values, default JSON-looking string preservation, and non-finite JSON number rejection.

Observed result after fix: 3 test files passed, 110 tests passed; format, lint, docs MDX, and Codex review are clean.

What was not tested: a live installed user config under  was not mutated; the behavior is covered with temporary JSONC fixtures through the same  handler used by the CLI.
